calculated_metrics.confidence_note: Cost per outcome calculation is not available. This organisation conducts significant multi-domain work (e.g., human education, environmental conservation). Dividing the total budget solely by quantified animal outcomes would artificially inflate the cost and misrepresent their financial efficiency.

details.formula: Calculation aborted due to multi-domain operations.

calculated_metrics.name: Cost Per Outcome (USD)

calculated_metrics.confidence_note: IES is a Back-of-the-Envelope Calculation (BOTEC) to standardize impact across interventions and species. It relies on philosophical and empirical assumptions.

breakdown.metric_name: Companion Animals Supported (via case management hub)

calculated_metrics.name: Impact Equivalency Score (IES)

details.criteria: Pass: <= 2 years of expenditure. | Warn: 2-5 years. | Fail: > 5 years.
Pass: >= 6 months of operating expenses. | Warn: 3-6 months. | Fail: < 3 months.
Pass: At least one intervention is classified as Tier 1 (Systemic Change) or Tier 2 (Prevention & Scalable Care). | Warn: All verifiable interventions are Tier 3 (Direct Care & Indirect Action).

details.formula: Mapping of verified intervention types to the Intervention Leverage Tier framework.

details.calculation: A quantified counterfactual baseline was provided.
details.criteria: Pass: At least one metric has a quantified counterfactual baseline. | Warn: A qualitative baseline is provided, but lacks quantified data. | Fail: No valid counterfactual baseline is provided.
details.elaboration: Quote: 'Every adoption at Animal Friends reflects our deep commitment to creating matches that honor second chances and lasting happiness for pets and people alike.'
details.formula: Presence of a quantified counterfactual baseline with a source quote.

details.calculation: Financials with income breakdown are missing.
details.criteria: Pass: Government grants are < 80% of total income. | Fail: Government grants are >= 80% of total income.

details.calculation: Operates in a low-neglectedness / saturated area (Companion Animals: 100%).
details.criteria: Pass: >= 50% of beneficiaries are high-neglectedness (farmed/wild). | Warn: 1-49% are high-neglectedness. | Fail: 0% are high-neglectedness.
details.formula: Proportional analysis of beneficiary populations (farmed/wild vs. companion)

details.calculation: No disclosure of unintended negative impacts found. This is the industry norm.
details.criteria: Bonus: Organisation self-reported on unintended negative impacts. | Not Disclosed: No disclosure found.
details.elaboration: This check rewards epistemic humility. Non-disclosure is standard and not a failure.
details.formula: Check for self-reported unintended consequences or failures.

details.calculation: No disclosure of euthanasia or live-release rates found.
details.criteria: Bonus: Euthanasia/live-release data is disclosed. | Not Disclosed: No disclosure found. | N/A: Not applicable for non-sheltering organisations.
details.elaboration: This check rewards transparency for a sensitive operational metric.
details.formula: For direct rescue orgs, check for euthanasia/live-release rate disclosure.

Our Mission To rescue, rehabilitate and rehome animals in crisis, ensure healthy pets through education, advocacy and affordable services, and inspire a community where the animal-human bond is celebrated and nurtured.

metrics.context_qualifier: Animals adopted into new homes

source.quote: Every adoption at Animal Friends reflects our deep commitment to creating matches that honor second chances and lasting happiness for pets and people alike.

metrics.context_qualifier: Spay & neuter surgeries performed at clinic

source.quote: the clinic offered free spay and neuter surgeries to City of Pittsburgh residents while the city's own spay and neuter program was on hold.

metrics.context_qualifier: Performed within shelter

source.quote: In the medical suite, one of our shelter vets completes a routine, yet vital procedure to help reduce overpopulation and make a difference in the community.

metrics.context_qualifier: Animals placed in foster homes

source.quote: Our foster families play a critical role, providing individualized care for animals who need more support than a shelter can offer, including nursing mothers and litters, bottle-fed babies, animals recovering from medical procedures, those working through behavior training and pets in hospice care.

metrics.context_qualifier: Admissions to shelter

source.quote: From the instant an animal arrives—whether as a stray, an owner surrender, a transfer from another shelter or a case handled by our Humane Investigations department – LSO shapes their journey.

metrics.context_qualifier: Overall live release rate for animals in care
Animals rehomed through home-to-home program

source.quote: *Animal Friends' home-to-home rehoming program offers an alternative to owner surrender, empowering families to help rehome their pets while they remain at home and enabling us to support more animals even when our kennels are full.

metrics.context_qualifier: Animals adopted by veterans through AFFV program

source.quote: AFFV is a comprehensive program designed to strengthen that bond. We help veterans find the right pet match, offer no cost adoptions for dogs, cats and rabbits, and provide ongoing support—from food and veterinary assistance to behavior guidance and emergency boarding in crisis situations — so that veterans and their pets can stay together, healthy and thriving.

metrics.context_qualifier: Companion animals supported through case management hub inquiries

source.quote: This strategy was strengthened in June with the launch of a centralized case management hub designed specifically for animal welfare organizations.

metrics.metric_name: Companion Animals Supported (via case management hub)

source.quote: Since its launch on June 1, it has helped the team respond to 440 inquiries, supporting the care of more than 812 companion animals.

metrics.context_qualifier: Pounds of pet food distributed to families

source.quote: Chow Wagon & Pet Retention The Art of Community Advocacy

metrics.context_qualifier: Families assisted with pet retention services

source.quote: Pet support services expanded as well through a partnership with a local grooming shop offering free nail trims at three community clinics.

metrics.context_qualifier: Administered at clinic

source.quote: 2025 was a year of meaningful growth for the clinic, driven by both expanded capacity and increasing community need.

metrics.context_qualifier: Administered at community vaccine clinics

source.quote: Community Programs and Clinic Services partnered to expand free, in-community vaccination clinics, increasing from four in 2024 to six in 2025.
4,863 Vaccines Administered Over Five Vaccine Clinics

metrics.context_qualifier: Administered within shelter

source.quote: Our medical team stands as guardians of health and well-being, delivering care that spans routine vaccines and spay/neuter procedures to infectious diseases and complex surgical interventions.

metrics.context_qualifier: Clinic services appointments

source.quote: 2025 was a year of meaningful growth for the clinic, driven by both expanded capacity and increasing community need.

metrics.context_qualifier: Special surgical procedures performed at clinic

source.quote: Testing revealed a severe intestinal blockage that required immediate surgery, and Bean's prognosis was guarded.

metrics.context_qualifier: Humane investigations cases resolved by HSPO

source.quote: In 2025, Animal Friends' Humane Investigations team practiced the quiet art of protection — carefully restoring a vital community service while never stepping away from animals in need.

metrics.context_qualifier: Individuals receiving guidance/resources for animals in crisis

source.quote: Even without a Humane Society Police Officer (HSPO) for much of the year, the department provided guidance, resources and referrals that helped 226 individuals secure proper care for animals in crisis.
provided guidance, resources and referrals that helped 226 individuals secure proper care for animals in crisis.

metrics.context_qualifier: Therapeutic interactions between animals and people

source.quote: Therapets and PALS (Pet-Assisted Literacy Services) continued to demonstrate the profound emotional impact animals can have.

metrics.context_qualifier: Individuals participating in humane education programs

source.quote: Last year, Humane Education expanded its reach and deepened its impact through school programs, scouting, family education, community events and 27 summer camps serving nearly 200 campers.

metrics.context_qualifier: Individuals participating in Animal Friends University programs

source.quote: Animal Friends University (AFU) continued to lead in progressive, accessible education, offering a wide range of engaging classes and workshops to the public for dogs, cats, rabbits and the people who love them.

metrics.context_qualifier: Interactions with veterans for pet support

source.quote: At Animal Friends, we understand how much companion animals mean to veterans, helping them stay active, engaged, supported and moving forward every day.

metrics.context_qualifier: Certified animal-assisted therapy teams

source.quote: Our certified Therapets teams bring the healing power of the animal-human bond to hospitals, schools, senior living communities, hospice facilities and libraries bringing comfort and affection to people in need.

metrics.context_qualifier: Dogs participating in New Leash program

source.quote: In 2025, Animal Friends continued to grow its prison-based dog training initiatives through The New Leash Prison Program, which brings together partnerships with correctional facilities under a shared mission.

metrics.context_qualifier: Volunteer hours contributed to the mission

source.quote: Volunteers are the heart of Animal Friends, and their impact in 2025 was remarkable.
More than 1,000 active volunteers contributed 301,063 hours to our mission

metrics.context_qualifier: Volunteer hours specifically for foster care

source.quote: Our foster families play a critical role, providing individualized care for animals who need more support than a shelter can offer, including nursing mothers and litters, bottle-fed babies, animals recovering from medical procedures, those working through behavior training and pets in hospice care.

metrics.context_qualifier: Active volunteers supporting the organization

source.quote: We hosted 19 orientations and welcomed 303 new volunteers, and partnered with 31 organizations for Days of Service, strengthening community connections while supporting our animals and programs.
